message:
feat(theme): Implement Theme system for named styles (Python Rich parity)

Port Python Rich's theme system for named style lookups, enabling style
references by name (e.g., "rule.line", "table.header") instead of inline
style definitions:

Theme System (src/theme.rs):
- Theme struct containing named style registry
- LazyLock-based DEFAULT_STYLES initialization from TSV file
- Theme::get_style() for named style lookup
- Theme::from_style_definitions() for custom theme creation
- Support for theme inheritance (inherit=true merges with defaults)

Default Styles (src/default_styles.tsv):
- Tab-separated file matching Python Rich's default_styles.py
- Includes all standard Rich styles: markup, repr, logging, tables, etc.
- Comments supported with # prefix
- Parsed at compile time for zero runtime overhead

Console Integration (src/lib.rs):
- Add Theme to Console configuration
- Console::builder().theme(theme) support
- Integrate theme lookup into style resolution path

Pretty Printer (src/renderables/pretty.rs):
- Initial pretty printer renderable implementation
- Uses theme styles for consistent repr formatting

Conformance Testing:
- Update python_rich.json fixtures with theme test cases
- Add parse_theme() to conformance_python.rs
- Update build_console() to accept optional theme parameter
- generate_fixtures.py: Add theme-aware fixture generation

README.md, FEATURE_PARITY.md, RICH_SPEC.md, PLAN_TO_PORT_RICH_TO_RUST.md:
- Document theme system implementation
- Update parity status

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -61,6 +65,13 @@ The conformance runner clears the standard Rich-related variables (`NO_COLOR`,
 `FORCE_COLOR`, `TERM`, `COLORTERM`) before applying per-case overrides to avoid
 leaking host environment into fixtures.
 
+### Theme Overrides
+
+Cases may specify a `theme` object to initialize the console theme:
+
+- `styles`: mapping of style name → style definition (e.g. `"bold red"`)
+- `inherit`: if true, inherit Python Rich default styles and overlay `styles`
+
 ### Normalization Rules
 
 To ensure deterministic comparisons:

 def render_case(case: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, str]:
     options = merge_render_options(case)
     env = build_env(case)
+    theme_config = case.get("theme")
+    theme = None
+    if theme_config:
+        styles = theme_config.get("styles", {})
+        inherit = theme_config.get("inherit", True)
+        theme = Theme(styles, inherit=inherit)
     console = Console(
         record=True,
         width=options.get("width"),
         color_system=options.get("color_system"),
         force_terminal=options.get("force_terminal"),
         force_jupyter=False,
+        theme=theme,
         legacy_windows=False,
         safe_box=True,
         emoji=True,
@@ -407,6 +421,7 @@ def main() -> int:

+            "theme": case.get("theme"),

             "expected": rendered,
             "notes": case.get("notes"),
